title: BigP3BCI Study E — 6x6 checkerboard (8 healthy subjects)
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 8 healthy subjects performing a P300-based brain-computer interface task using a 6x6 checkerboard visual stimulus paradigm. Participants completed a single session of target and non-target visual perception trials recorded at 256 Hz using 16-channel EEG with a standard 10-20 montage. The data represents a subset of the BigP3BCI study, the largest public P300 BCI dataset, and is intended for BCI speller applications and P300 event-related potential detection research.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at 256 Hz sampling rate using 16 channels arranged in a standard 10-20 montage with g.USBamp hardware (g.tec). Subjects performed a P300 speller task with a 6x6 checkerboard visual stimulus paradigm in a laboratory setting with online feedback. Trial intervals were 0-1.0 seconds, with Target and NonTarget event classes. Data were processed using HED 8.4.0 event annotations and feature extraction focused on P300 ERP detection.
